Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting significant renal dysfunction and possible diabetic ketoacidosis. Elevated ALT indicates some hepatic involvement. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, further supporting renal impairment. Immediate clinical evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and metabolic derangements is recommended.
